content:
First, one checks that “\(\triangle ABC\sim\triangle ACD\)” forces the correspondence 
\[
(A,B,C)\longmapsto(A,C,D),
\]
so that
\[
\frac{AB}{AC}=\frac{AC}{AD}
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\frac{4}{6}=\frac{6}{AD}
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
AD=9
\]
and likewise \(CD=7.5\).  One convenient placement is
\[
A=(0,0),\quad C=(6,0),
\]
and, since \(\cos\angle CAB=\tfrac{9}{16}\), one finds
\[
B=\bigl(4\cos\theta,4\sin\theta\bigr)
=\Bigl(\tfrac{9}{4},\tfrac{5\sqrt7}{4}\Bigr),
\]
while from the similarity the point \(D\) lies on the line through \(A\) making the same angle below the \(x\)–axis, at distance \(9\); one checks
\[
D=\Bigl(\tfrac{81}{16},-\tfrac{45\sqrt7}{16}\Bigr),
\]
and indeed \(|\,AD|=9,\;|\,CD|=7.5\).  

Next \(P\) lies on the ray \(DA\) past \(A\), so
\[
P=A+t(A-D)=-\,tD,\quad t>0.
\]
The condition
\[
\angle BDC=\angle ACP
\]
is equivalent to
\[
\frac{(B-D)\cdot(C-D)}{\|B-D\|\;\|C-D\|}
=\frac{(A-C)\cdot(P-C)}{\|A-C\|\;\|P-C\|},
\]
which one checks reduces to a single quadratic in \(t\).  The only admissible solution is
\[
t=\frac49,
\]
hence
\[
P=-\frac49\,D,\quad
DP=\Bigl\|P-D\Bigr\|
=\Bigl\|-\tfrac{4}{9}D-D\Bigr\|
=\tfrac{13}{9}\,\|D\|
=\tfrac{13}{9}\cdot9
=13,
\]
and therefore
\[
\boxed{DP^2=169}.
\]
